UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents a user's profile information within the NEXO application. This entity stores personal details, display preferences, and online status.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity.

numericId.description: A unique 7-9 digit numeric ID assigned to the user, displayed on the 'Me' screen.

email.description: The user's email address, used for identification and communication (not for authentication credentials).

displayName.description: The name chosen by the user to be displayed publicly.

profilePictureUrl.description: URL to the user's profile picture.

statusMessage.description: A custom message set by the user to indicate their current status or mood.

lastOnlineAt.description: Timestamp indicating the last time the user was actively online.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user profile was created.

ChatConversation.title: ChatConversation
ChatConversation.description: Represents a single chat conversation, which can be one-on-one or a group chat.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ChatConversation entity.

type.description: The type of the chat conversation (e.g., 'one-on-one' or 'group').

name.description: The name of the group chat (null for one-on-one chats).

creatorId.description: Reference to UserProfile, identifying the user who created the group chat. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N ChatConversation)

participantIds.description: References to UserProfile entities that are participants in this conversation. (Relationship: ChatConversation N:N UserProfile)

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the chat conversation was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating the last activity or message in this conversation.

lastMessageId.description: Reference to the most recent Message in this conversation for quick display. (Relationship: ChatConversation 1:1 Message)

Message.title: Message
Message.description: Represents a single message sent within a chat conversation.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Message entity.

chatId.description: Reference to ChatConversation, indicating which conversation this message belongs to. (Relationship: ChatConversation 1:N Message)

senderId.description: Reference to UserProfile, identifying the sender of the message. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Message)

content.description: The actual text content of the message.

sentAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the message was sent.

readByRecipientIds.description: References to UserProfile entities who have read this message. (Relationship: Message N:N UserProfile)

CallHistory.title: CallHistory
CallHistory.description: Records details of voice and video calls made through the application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the CallHistory entity.

callerId.description: Reference to UserProfile, identifying the user who initiated the call. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N CallHistory)

calleeIds.description: References to UserProfile entities who participated in the call. (Relationship: CallHistory N:N UserProfile)

startTime.description: Timestamp indicating when the call started.

endTime.description: Timestamp indicating when the call ended. Null if the call is ongoing.

durationSeconds.description: The duration of the call in seconds. Null if the call is ongoing.

definition.description: Stores individual user profile information. Access is typically restricted to the owning user for write operations, while certain fields (e.g., displayName, profilePictureUrl, statusMessage) may be publicly readable by authenticated users for discovery and chat display. The `userId` in the path corresponds to `request.auth.uid`, establishing path-based ownership.

Represents a distinct chat conversation, either one-on-one or a group. Access to this document and its subcollections (messages) is governed by the `participantIds` field, which lists all user UIDs involved in the conversation. This field is crucial for Authorization Independence, enabling direct checks in security rules without parent document reads. Includes denormalized 'participantIds' array for authorization independence.

params.name: chatId
params.description: The unique identifier for the chat conversation.

definition.description: Stores individual messages within a specific chat conversation. Each message document includes a denormalized `participantIds` array, copied from its parent `ChatConversation` document. This allows security rules to independently verify if `request.auth.uid` is a participant for reading or writing messages, upholding Authorization Independence. Includes denormalized 'participantIds' array for authorization independence.

params.name: chatId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent chat conversation.

params.name: messageId
params.description: The unique identifier for the message.

definition.description: Records details of voice and video calls. Access is restricted to `callerId` and any `calleeIds` listed within the document. These fields are directly available on the document, providing Authorization Independence for rule evaluation. Includes 'callerId' and 'calleeIds' for authorization.

params.name: callId
params.description: The unique identifier for the call history record.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed with a strong emphasis on Authorization Independence, Structural Segregation, and consistent Access Modeling to ensure secure, scalable, and debuggable security rules. Each collection's security posture is homogeneous, meaning all documents within a given collection follow the same access patterns, simplifying rule logic.

Authorization Independence is achieved primarily through denormalization. For `ChatConversation` documents, the `participantIds` array directly within each conversation document serves as the membership map. This allows security rules to check `request.auth.uid in resource.data.participantIds` for read/write access to a conversation without requiring any `get()` operations on a parent or external document. Similarly, for `CallHistory` documents, the `callerId` and `calleeIds` fields are present on each document, enabling direct authorization checks.

Crucially, for `Message` documents, which are nested within `ChatConversation`s (`/chatConversations/{chatId}/messages/{messageId}`), Authorization Independence is maintained by denormalizing the `participantIds` from the parent `ChatConversation` directly into each `Message` document. This means a message document will contain a `participantIds` array. Consequently, rules for messages can check `request.auth.uid in resource.data.participantIds` without needing to perform a `get()` call to the parent `ChatConversation` document. This design choice is critical for supporting atomic operations (like creating a message in a transaction) and for simplifying security rules by eliminating cross-document reads for authorization.

Queryability and Authorization (QAPs) are robustly supported. For `ChatConversation` and `CallHistory` collections, users can perform `list` operations filtered by fields like `participantIds` or `callerId`/`calleeIds` respectively. Because the authorization context (e.g., `participantIds`) is denormalized directly onto each document, Firestore security rules can enforce that only documents where `request.auth.uid` is a participant are returned in a `list` query. This prevents over-fetching data and ensures that security rules act as query enforcers rather than client-side filters, leading to efficient and secure data access.
